questions: The batch editor's `useSelection` hook signature takes `onSelectionChange?: (hasSelection: boolean) => void` — a boolean, not the actual IDs. The bridge hook needs the actual selected IDs. The plan wires the bridge inside `useShotImageManagerSelectionOrderController` which has access to `selection.selectedIds` directly. Is there a reason the batch editor only surfaces a boolean externally? Should we also expose the IDs upstream, or is wiring inside the container sufficient?

The `registerPeerClear` slot is singular — only one callback can be registered at a time. If both the timeline and batch editor are mounted simultaneously (e.g., the timeline is visible while the batch editor is in a different tab/mode), the last one to mount wins. In practice, are these views mutually exclusive (only one visible at a time), or could both be mounted?

Should selecting images in the travel timeline/batch editor also clear the GenerationsPane gallery selection (it will, via `selectGalleryItems` calling `peerClearRef`), and should selecting in the GenerationsPane gallery clear the travel timeline/batch editor selection (it will, via the registered peer-clear callback)? This mutual exclusivity is the existing pattern from the video-editor — confirm this is the desired behavior.

success_criteria.criterion: Selecting images in the video travel timeline causes them to appear as attachments in the global AgentChat component
Selecting images in the video travel batch editor causes them to appear as attachments in the global AgentChat component
Selecting images in the image generation gallery continues to show them as attachments in AgentChat (no regression)
Deselecting all images in any surface removes the attachments from AgentChat
Selecting in one surface clears selection in other surfaces (mutual exclusivity via peer-clear)
Sending a message in AgentChat clears the gallery selection and thus the local surface selection
The bridge hook is reusable and under ~50 lines — no over-abstraction
No changes to existing selection UX behavior in any surface (click, toggle, range-select, drag-to-select all continue working)
Bridge hook unmount cleanup: clears gallery selection and unregisters peer-clear
Manual smoke test across all three surfaces confirms end-to-end flow

assumptions: The image generation gallery (GenerationsPaneGallery) is already fully wired to GallerySelectionContext and needs no changes — confirmed by code reading.
The video travel timeline and batch editor are never both actively interactive at the same time (they're different modes of the same ShotEditor), so sharing the single registerPeerClear slot is safe.
GenerationRow fields (imageUrl, type, generation_id, primary_variant_id) provide sufficient data to build GallerySelectionMeta without additional queries.
The bridge hook should use useGallerySelectionOptional() (not the throwing version) so it degrades gracefully if context is ever absent.
Unmounting the travel-between-images page should clear any gallery selection that was set by its bridge, so AgentChat doesn't show stale attachments.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
